response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished full-stack and distributed-systems engineer with strong TypeScript/Node.js credentials, but he is a poor fit for this C++-centric industrial automation role. His C++ exposure is limited to approximately six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a university thesis project; C++ does not even appear in his current skills list. He has no documented experience with industrial protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850), no background in industrial automation or IoT platforms, and no familiarity with the IEC 62443 secure development standards that are central to this position. While his distributed-systems and high-performance engineering work is impressive, it does not translate to the domain-specific depth this role demands.
strengths: Proven high-performance, high-throughput systems engineering (millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby using async I/O and low-latency data pipelines)
Node.js and TypeScript depth across multiple production environments aligns with the advantageous web-stack skills (React, Node.js)
Consistent application of agile engineering practices — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development — across a long career
concerns: C++ proficiency is marginal: roughly six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a thesis project; absent from his skills list and all recent roles
No industrial automation, IoT, or OT-domain experience; zero exposure to Modbus, MQTT, OPC UA, IEC 61850, DNP 3.0, or comparable industrial protocols
No evidence of secure development lifecycle (SDL) practice or IEC 62443 industrial-cybersecurity standards knowledge, which are core to the role
